cic-ingestion (master).status: DIVERGED from origin, not a plain fast-forward
cic-ingestion (master).origin_only_note: all 7 are CI workflow Node-version pinning, no source conflict expected
cic-ingestion (master).local_only_span: 2026-06-29 through 2026-07-14, Phase 2 through Phase 7 feature work

tweetable: Week of Jul 7: 99 commits (solo), 122k net LOC, marketplace Phase 9 waves A/B/C shipped, cic-ingestion build fully broken then fixed (invalid tsconfig blocked all 35 tests), chat-agent quality-routing pipeline built from scratch. Found: cic-ingestion diverged 50 commits from origin since Jun 29, unresolved.
session_findings: cic-ingestion tsconfig.json had ignoreDeprecations:"6.0" (invalid value, TS5103) — blocked 100% of builds and all 35 test suites from even running. One-line fix (moduleResolution: node10) restored 358/359 passing. Root cause: commit e5fe7516 silenced a deprecation warning with a bad value instead of fixing the underlying moduleResolution rename.
uuid@14 is ESM-only (no CJS build) — broke 4 more test suites under ts-jest. Fixed via a moduleNameMapper shim to crypto.randomUUID, not a version downgrade.

AutonomyAPIServer.ts (live entrypoint, wired into server.ts) is unbuildable: uses explicit .ts import extensions (needs Node's native strip-types runtime) while the rest of the repo builds via tsc/node10 resolution — two incompatible runtime models in one repo. Also depends on node-cron (never installed) and src/resilience/{hardeningOrchestrator,circuitBreaker,rateLimiter}.ts (source confirmed permanently gone: checked all branches, full git history, whole c:/dev tree — only stale compiled dist/ remains). Documented as a known gap, not fixed.

services/chat-agent and services/torquequery were both dist-only (compiled output committed nowhere, no source, no package.json) — same orphaned-build pattern as the missing resilience modules. Built chat-agent's model-selection pipeline from scratch (quality-metrics-driven, mirrors cic-ingestion's existing routingCostSignals.ts bias-tier convention).

cic-ingestion has been silently diverging from its own origin/master since 2026-06-29 — not just unpushed, genuinely forked. 50 local-only commits vs 7 origin-only (CI workflow fixes). A plain git push will be rejected; needs an explicit merge/rebase decision.
recommendations: Resolve cic-ingestion divergence before it grows further — merge or rebase onto origin/master's 7 CI-only commits (low conflict risk, but needs a deliberate choice, not a habit-fix).

The 'push as last command of session' habit (set 2026-07-12) is not holding for cic-ingestion — it's a separate repo from c:/dev and easy to forget. Consider a session-end checklist that lists ALL repos with uncommitted/unpushed state, not just the primary one.

Two repos this week (cic-ingestion, chat-agent, torquequery) had dist-only orphaned build output with no source ever committed. Worth an audit across services/ for more instances before they're needed under time pressure.
